pubmed_links: [Magno et al. (2019) Alzheimer's disease phospholipase C-gamma-2 (PLCG2) protective variant is a functional hypermorph](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/30711010/); [Tsai et al. (2023) Genetic variants of phospholipase C-gamma-2 alter the phenotype and function of microglia and confer differential risk for Alzheimer's disease](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37659412/)
summary: PLCG2, expressed selectively in brain microglia, harbors variants with opposing effects on Alzheimer's disease risk: the protective P522R hypermorph enhances microglial phagocytosis and immune function, while the loss-of-function M28L variant exacerbates amyloid burden through impaired microglial clearance; though primarily linked to neurodegeneration rather than classic neuropsychiatric disorders, its central role in microglial signaling and neuroinflammation is relevant to broader neuropathology. (emerging evidence)
